page: Amazon Web Services m6gd.large is a general-purpose instance powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. Built on the 64-bit ARM architecture, this instance features 2 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing a total of 2 vCPUs. The processor is equipped with 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Additionally, the instance includes 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate modest performance characteristics. In stress-ng testing, the single-core CPU performance scored 1519.39, placing it in the average tier, while the multi-core CPU performance reached 3013.17, which is categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 117.17 GB/sec and a write large block speed of 19.3 GB/sec. Geekbench testing yielded a single-core score of 958.0 and a multi-core score of 1752.0. For database and key-value operations, the instance achieved 1289.39 in Passmark database operations and 2.28 million operations per second in Redis SET operations.

The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage provides a distinct advantage for workloads requiring low-latency scratch space or temporary caching, offsetting the lower baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. However, the ARM64 architecture lacks support for x86-specific instruction sets such as AVX or AMX, which limits its suitability for heavy vector math or advanced matrix operations. Given the balanced resource allocation and modest multi-core performance, this instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, lightweight web serving, development environments, and applications that can leverage local SSD storage without requiring intensive multi-threaded compute.
description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.large is a general-purpose ARM64 instance powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores, 8.0 GB of memory, and 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark evaluations show average single-core CPU performance but weaker multi-core and memory write speeds. Lacking x86-specific vector instructions, it is not designed for high-performance computing. Instead, its balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and local SSD storage make it a cost-efficient choice for lightweight web serving, database caching, development environments, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from low-latency local scratch space.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.large is a general-purpose ARM64 instance featuring 2 Graviton vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 118 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, suitable for lightweight web serving and caching.

tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for balanced, low-latency workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor
Equipped with 2 dedicated physical cores and 8.0 GB of system memory
Includes 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Delivers 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for lightweight web serving, caching, and development environments
